mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months where FEDFUNDS declines, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in >60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio < 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which boosts equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, with a hit rate below 45% over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply-side slack, which immediately pressures equity prices and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >50% of months where INDPRO declines, or the signal shows zero predictive power (Rank IC = 0) across all tested regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 202.0 indicate entrenched inflation that forces monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the threshold, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance (45% hit rate).

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >50% of months where PAYEMS increases, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining real GDP growth and rising personal consumption expenditures signals unsustainable consumer leverage that precedes a demand collapse, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns, with positive 60-day returns occurring in >60% of instances, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls confirms robust supply-side capacity utilization, which validates earnings forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of triggered instances, or the joint threshold condition yields a Rank IC below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.
